activities_projects.title: Observation with a Magnifying Glass
activities_projects.objective: Understand how magnifying glasses help in observing tiny objects closely
steps: Take a magnifying glass and observe small objects such as leaves, sand, or insects
Note down the details you can see clearly through the magnifying glass
Compare how the object looks with and without the magnifying glass
Write your observations in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how magnification helps in detailed observation of small objects
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of observation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Picture Analysis Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify the purpose of magnifying tools in daily life
steps: Observe the pictures given in the textbook
Discuss what the people in the pictures are doing
Identify why they are using a magnifying glass
Write your answers in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the practical uses of magnifying instruments

activities_projects.title: Poem Summary Writing
steps: Read the poem 'Magnifying Glass' carefully
Identify the main idea of the poem
Write a short summary explaining how the magnifying glass reveals hidden details
Share the summary with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve reading comprehension and summarizing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognize poetic devices used in the poem
steps: Read the poem again carefully
Identify examples of simile, metaphor, and alliteration
Write the examples in your notebook
Explain their meanings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how poets use literary devices to create imagery

activities_projects.title: Creative Drawing Activity
steps: Draw an object as seen normally
Draw the same object as it might appear through a magnifying glass
Label the details that become visible through magnification
Present your drawing to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how magnification reveals hidden details
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity in drawing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Improve vocabulary and understanding of new words
steps: Identify difficult words from the poem
Find their meanings using a dictionary
Write the meanings in your notebook
Use each word in a sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve vocabulary and contextual usage
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and sentence formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Observation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of careful observation in science and daily life
steps: Form small groups
Discuss how magnifying glasses help scientists
Share examples of tools that magnify objects
Present your ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of observation in scientific discovery
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Collective Nouns Matching Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand collective nouns used in English
steps: Read the collective noun pairs in the exercise
Match the correct phrases such as 'a swarm of locusts'
Write the correct answers in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn correct usage of collective nouns

activities_projects.title: Idioms with 'Eye' Activity
steps: Read the idioms related to 'eye'
Match them with their meanings
Write example sentences using the idioms
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand figurative language and idioms
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage of idioms in sentences (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing: Wonders Through a Lens
activities_projects.objective: Develop creative thinking and writing skills
steps: Imagine you have a magnifying glass
Write a short paragraph about what tiny wonders you would like to observe
Describe how they look when magnified
Share your writing with the teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance imagination and descriptive writing skills
